title: plan
description: An ad-hoc, executable plan the workbench planner proposes for a concrete piece of work: one goal, exactly one executable step, and a verify gate that proves the step landed. A strict subset of cherny-loop's gate_plan.json (single step, single verify) so an accepted plan can be handed straight to cherny-loop when iteration is wanted. The operator accepts it as-is (accept_plan) or refines it (free text → the work sink re-dispatches the planner with the prior plan); apply runs the step then runs verify and routes on a real pass/fail verdict.

goal.description: One line: the contract this plan satisfies, e.g. 'Migrate repo-local issues/ to GitHub issues.'

step.description: Exactly one executable step (single-step is the v1 scope; multi-step is cherny-loop's job).

kind.description: How the step is carried out. 'run' / 'agent' both dispatch the landing_agent prompted with the ACCEPTED plan as its instruction (not a fresh re-prose).

description.description: What the step does, concretely, e.g. 'Create a GitHub issue per file in issues/, mapping frontmatter.'

mutating.description: True ⇒ apply holds for the operator's write-mode grant before the effect lands.

verify.description: The gate that proves the step is done. A subset of gate_plan's gate config.

mode.description: script: a Starlark verify script (default, preferred); agent: an adversarial gate_reviewer; hybrid: both.

script.description: Path to the Starlark verify script (relative to the story), e.g. 'verify/issues_migrated.star'. Required for script/hybrid; empty for pure agent gates.

inputs.description: Typed inputs for the verify script, validated by the script's .star.yaml sidecar, e.g. { expected_min: 3 }.

reason.description: Why this gate proves the goal is done, e.g. 'Passes iff GitHub lists >= expected_min open issues matching the local set.'
